sourceMovie.title: John Wick: Chapter 4
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: John Wick: Chapter 4 works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum draws from the same action well as John Wick: Chapter 4, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2019 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Transporter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Transporter (2002) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that John Wick: Chapter 4 later expanded. If John Wick: Chapter 4 resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: Run All Night
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of John Wick: Chapter 4 — that dark edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into Run All Night (2015). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The International
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The International (2009) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps John Wick: Chapter 4 rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Dead Pool
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Dead Pool (1988) is the kind of film John Wick: Chapter 4 fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Supremacy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Bourne Supremacy shares John Wick: Chapter 4's gift for action — the 2004 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Road to Perdition
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes John Wick: Chapter 4 compelling — the thriller, the crime — shows up in Road to Perdition (2002) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: The Killer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The thriller energy of John Wick: Chapter 4 is alive in The Killer (2023), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Polar
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Polar (2019) operates in the same action as John Wick: Chapter 4, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Unleashed
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Unleashed (2005) belongs to the same conversation as John Wick: Chapter 4 — both anchor their action in crime emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

faq.question: What should I watch after John Wick: Chapter 4?
faq.answer: Try John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum, The Transporter, Run All Night—each shares DNA with John Wick: Chapter 4 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is John Wick: Chapter 4?
faq.answer: John Wick: Chapter 4 (2023) is best described as Action, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is John Wick: Chapter 4 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "John Wick Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
